description: Dominicans Don't Play (DDP) is a Dominican-American street gang formed in Manhattan, New York, in 1991. Known for using machetes and knives, they are involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. The gang operates across New York City, New Jersey, and internationally, with ties to Colombian cartels for cocaine distribution.

country_links.evidence_quote: Spanish police in Madrid noted a growing gang presence of the DDP. Spanish police report 130 documented DDP members, making them the largest in Spain.
country_links.context: The DDP has a documented presence in Spain, with 130 members reported by Spanish police, making it the largest and most violent street gang in Spain.
